events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves crane operations near power lines, which is a critical safety issue under OSHA 1926.1400. Power line clearance is one of the most dangerous aspects of crane operations. OSHA 1926.1408 specifically addresses power line safety. The minimum clearance distances are critical - for lines up to 50kV, minimum clearance is 10 feet; for higher voltages, clearances increase.

If power line clearance is being observed as a field condition during active crane work, this is likely a non-compliant situation that needs immediate action. Working near energized power lines without proper clearance distances, spotters, or protective measures is a critical hazard.

Given the scenario mentions "power line clearance" as the observed field condition, this suggests the crane is operating near power lines and clearance may be insufficient or not properly managed.

Under OSHA 1926.1408, cranes operating near power lines must maintain minimum clearance distances or implement specific protective measures like barricades, warning lines, spotters, or de-energizing the lines.

This is a fail situation with critical risk if clearance is not being maintained properly.

  "findings": [
    "Crane operating within minimum clearance distance of energized power lines without documented encroachment prevention measures",
    "No evidence of dedicated spotter, warning line/barricade system, or de-energization/grounding permit in effect",
    "Power line clearance not verified against OSHA 1926.1408 minimum distance table for the line voltage present"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt crane operations immediately and lower or retract the boom to a safe resting position",
    "Identify line voltage and confirm required minimum clearance per OSHA 1926.1408 Table A (10 ft for up to 50kV, increasing with voltage)",
    "Implement encroachment prevention: erect warning lines/barricades at the minimum clearance boundary, assign a dedicated spotter, or coordinate with utility for de-energization/grounding",
    "Verify clearance distance with the site safety officer and document the control measures before resuming lifts",
    "Confirm load chart compliance for all planned picks once clearance controls are in place"
  ],
  "rationale": "Operating a crane near energized power lines without verified minimum clearance and active encroachment prevention is a critical OSHA 1926.1408 violation and an immediate electrocution/arc-flash hazard. This is not a common shortcut that is tolerable; it is a stop-work condition. The operation must fail until clearance controls are documented and enforced.",

    "OSHA 1926.1408 - Power line safety (up to 350 kV) - minimum clearance distances and encroachment prevention",
    "OSHA 1926.1400 - Cranes and Derricks in Construction - general applicability and definitions",
    "OSHA 1926.1408 Table A - minimum clearance distances by line voltage"
  ]
}
